perturbation.original_text: As a condition to the Company’s disclosure of the Confidential Information to the Recipient, the Recipient hereby covenants and agrees that all Confidential Information will be kept confidential by the Recipient and will not, without the prior written consent of the Company, be disclosed by the Recipient, in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, and will not be used by the Recipient, directly or indirectly, for any purpose other than facilitating discussion with the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: As a condition to the Company’s disclosure of the Confidential Information to the Recipient, the Recipient hereby covenants and agrees that all Confidential Information will be kept confidential by the Recipient and will not, without the prior written consent of the Company, be disclosed by the Recipient, in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, and will not be used by the Recipient, directly or indirectly, for any purpose other than facilitating discussion with the Company. However, the Recipient is allowed to disclose such information for its internal business purposes.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "However, the Recipient is allowed to disclose such information for its internal business purposes," a contradiction arises. The original sentence states that Confidential Information will not be disclosed without the Company's consent, while the added sentence permits disclosure for the Recipient's internal business purposes. This creates an ambiguity regarding the extent to which the Recipient is bound by the non-disclosure covenant, especially since it's explicitly contradicting the non-disclosure of confidential information without the company's consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2.1 Non-Disclosure and Non-Use Covenants . As a condition to the Company’s disclosure of the Confidential Information to the Recipient, the Recipient hereby covenants and agrees that all Confidential Information will be kept confidential by the Recipient and will not, without the prior written consent of the Company, be disclosed by the Recipient, in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, and will not be used by the Recipient, directly or indirectly, for any purpose other than facilitating discussion with the Company. However, the Recipient is allowed to disclose such information for its internal business purposes. Moreover, the Recipient agrees to transmit the Confidential Information to only those Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of facilitating the Recipient’s discussion with the Company and who are informed of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and the terms of this Agreement. The Recipient hereby acknowledges that it is aware, and the Recipient agrees that it will advise its Representatives who are informed as to the matters which are the subject of this Agreement, that (i) the Confidential Information being furnished may contain or may itself be material, non-public information concerning the Company, and (ii) securities laws in the United States prohibit any person who has received material, non-public information concerning the Company or the matters which are the subject of this Agreement from purchasing or selling securities of the Company or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities. The Recipient agrees to not engage, directly or indirectly, in transactions in Company securities while in possession of Confidential 2 Information, and further agrees that is will be responsible for its compliance with federal and state securities laws. The Recipient will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or by its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds "However, the Recipient is allowed to disclose such information for its internal business purposes," which directly contradicts the earlier statement that "all Confidential Information will be kept confidential by the Recipient and will not, without the prior written consent of the Company, be disclosed by the Recipient." This creates a strong in-text contradiction within Section 2.1, as evidenced in the 'Contradicted Text' section, as well as the original modified text, directly contradicting the non-disclosure agreement.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will not, without the prior written consent of the Company, disclose the status of any discussions between the Parties, except as may be required by law or by the rules of any recognized stock exchange and then, if circumstances permit, only with prompt advance written notice to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient will not, without the prior written consent of the Company, disclose the status of any discussions between the Parties, except as may be required by law or by the rules of any recognized stock exchange and then, if circumstances permit, only with prompt advance written notice to the Company. The Recipient is encouraged to disclose such discussions with potential investors to facilitate fundraising.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly prohibits disclosing the status of discussions without prior written consent, except when required by law. The added sentence, "The Recipient is encouraged to disclose such discussions with potential investors to facilitate fundraising," directly contradicts this restriction. It creates an in-text contradiction by encouraging a behavior that was previously prohibited without consent, thus creating uncertainty whether the recipient can or cannot disclose, especially about fundraising.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2.2 Confidentiality of Discussions . The Recipient will not, without the prior written consent of the Company, disclose the status of any discussions between the Parties, except as may be required by law or by the rules of any recognized stock exchange and then, if circumstances permit, only with prompt advance written notice to the Company. The Recipient is encouraged to disclose such discussions with potential investors to facilitate fundraising. Any disclosure made pursuant to the prior sentence shall be no more extensive than is necessary to meet the minimum requirement imposed on the Person making such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds "The Recipient is encouraged to disclose such discussions with potential investors to facilitate fundraising." to a section that previously stated, "The Recipient will not, without the prior written consent of the Company, disclose the status of any discussions between the Parties..." This creates a direct contradiction. The original text prohibits disclosure without written consent, while the added sentence encourages disclosure, specifically regarding fundraising, thus making the recipient unsure if they can or cannot disclose without consent. The contradicted text, also Section 2.2, contains both of these statements, making the contradiction explicitly in-text and strong.

perturbation.original_text: The Confidential Information (except for Derivative Materials), and all copies thereof, will remain the absolute property of the Company and will be returned to the Company or destroyed (and such destruction confirmed in writing by an officer of the Recipient) without retaining any copies thereof immediately upon the Company’s request.
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidential Information (except for Derivative Materials), and all copies thereof, will remain the absolute property of the Company and will be returned to the Company or destroyed (and such destruction confirmed in writing by an officer of the Recipient) without retaining any copies thereof immediately upon the Company’s request. However, the Recipient may retain one copy of the Confidential Information for archival purposes, even after the Company's request for return or destruction.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "However, the Recipient may retain one copy of the Confidential Information for archival purposes, even after the Company's request for return or destruction," a contradiction is introduced. The original sentence requires the return or destruction of all copies of Confidential Information upon the Company's request, without any exceptions. The added sentence creates an exception, allowing the Recipient to retain one copy, directly conflicting with the earlier requirement to return/destroy *all* copies. There is an uncertainty if the recipient, should return or destroy all copies or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2.4 Document Disposition . The Confidential Information (except for Derivative Materials), and all copies thereof, will remain the absolute property of the Company and will be returned to the Company or destroyed (and such destruction confirmed in writing by an officer of the Recipient) without retaining any copies thereof immediately upon the Company’s request. However, the Recipient may retain one copy of the Confidential Information for archival purposes, even after the Company's request for return or destruction. Derivative Materials will be immediately destroyed at the request of the Company and such destruction will be confirmed to the Company in writing by an officer of the Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction because it adds an exception to the requirement to return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information upon the Company's request. The original text states that 'all copies thereof, will remain the absolute property of the Company and will be returned to the Company or destroyed... without retaining any copies thereof immediately upon the Company’s request.' The changed text adds 'However, the Recipient may retain one copy of the Confidential Information for archival purposes, even after the Company's request for return or destruction.' This creates a direct conflict, as the original text mandates the return/destruction of *all* copies, while the changed text allows for retaining one copy. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type because the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: The restrictions imposed on the disclosure of Confidential Information shall continue until the lesser of such time as the information disclosed hereunder is no longer Confidential Information and one (1) year from the date first written above, provided, that no such termination shall relieve Recipient from any liability relating to any prior breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The restrictions imposed on the disclosure of Confidential Information shall continue until the lesser of such time as the information disclosed hereunder is no longer Confidential Information and one (1) year from the date first written above, provided, that no such termination shall relieve Recipient from any liability relating to any prior breach of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, these restrictions terminate immediately upon a change in control of the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the restrictions on disclosing Confidential Information continue for one year. By adding "Notwithstanding the foregoing, these restrictions terminate immediately upon a change in control of the Recipient," a contradiction is created. The original clause implies a minimum duration of one year, while the addition allows for immediate termination under specific circumstances. This contradiction introduces uncertainty about the actual duration of the confidentiality restrictions. It is unclear if the recipient has to keep it for one year or upon the change of control, the confidentiality immediately ends.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2.7 Survival . The restrictions imposed on the disclosure of Confidential Information shall continue until the lesser of such time as the information disclosed hereunder is no longer Confidential Information and one (1) year from the date first written above, provided, that no such termination shall relieve Recipient from any liability relating to any prior breach of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, these restrictions terminate immediately upon a change in control of the Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the confidentiality restrictions last for at least one year. The changed text adds a clause allowing immediate termination upon a change of control, which contradicts the initial one-year restriction. This creates a strong and direct conflict, as both cannot be true: the restrictions cannot simultaneously last for one year and terminate immediately under certain conditions. The contradicted text highlights this conflict.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties will pay their own expenses with respect to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties will pay their own expenses with respect to this Agreement. However, the Company will reimburse the Recipient for all reasonable legal fees incurred in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that each party bears its own expenses. Adding "However, the Company will reimburse the Recipient for all reasonable legal fees incurred in connection with this Agreement" directly contradicts this. It creates an in-text contradiction about expense allocation, making it uncertain who is responsible for legal fees. It also contradicts the original meaning, making the enforcement ambigious.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 5.5 Expenses . The Parties will pay their own expenses with respect to this Agreement. However, the Company will reimburse the Recipient for all reasonable legal fees incurred in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states "The Parties will pay their own expenses with respect to this Agreement.", while the changed text adds "However, the Company will reimburse the Recipient for all reasonable legal fees incurred in connection with this Agreement." This creates a direct contradiction regarding who is responsible for legal fees. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, which means the Change introduced a contradiction by adding an exception to the rule that each party pays their own expenses.
